fields.description:
# ksqlDB Error Investigation -- Unified Incident Report

**Generated:** 2026-06-01T14:33:31.857Z

## Executive Summary

The ksqlDB errors are caused by a 90-minute MSK Kafka controller election storm on cluster c72-shared-services-msk.

comments.body:
<custom data-type="mention" data-id="id-0">@Zeljko Urban</custom>

Incident Report: Persistent Confluent Kafka Bootstrap Broker Disconnections -- localcore-service

## Executive Summary

The localcore-service consumer groups have been continuously failing to complete the Kafka bootstrap handshake against the Confluent Cloud broker lkc-5wggoq.dom4gl282wl.eu-central-1.aws.confluent.cloud:9092 for 30 days. The broker node ID remains id: -1 on every attempt. The root cause is a Confluent Cloud-layer SASL/TLS authentication failure. The most probable immediate trigger is that the Confluent API key or secret stored in SSM is invalid. A DNS failure would produce Unknown host, not terminated during authentication.

Confidence: 0.87

Root cause found: it's a DNS/network gap, not credentials.

localcore-service connects directly to Confluent Cloud at lkc-5wggoq.dom4gl282wl...:9092. From the oit-prd VPC (vpc-0cfdebf02337d59fd) that hostname resolves to non-routable Confluent-internal IPs (10.1.x.x), because the VPC has no Confluent Route53 resolver rule associated. Resolver associations are per-VPC and not transitive over the TGW.

The CUSTOMER_ASSIGNMENTS_KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS env var pointing at c72-shared-services-msk is vestigial. The empty MSK consumer groups are orphans from that retired path. MSK is not in the incident path.

Fix: associate resolver rule rslvr-rr-b8729ffeed604a26a (dom4gl282wl) with vpc-0cfdebf02337d59fd, codified in oit.terraform.

content.text: Devs confirmed this was parked and never fully tested so the fix I suggested should finish infra part and connection to CC should be possible from oit accounts
